name: TAYLOR DEVICES, INC.
summary: Taylor Devices, Inc., incorporated in New York in 1955, designs, develops, manufactures, and markets shock absorption, rate control, and energy storage devices. Products include Seismic Dampers, Fluidicshoks, Crane and Industrial Buffers, Self-Adjusting Shock Absorbers, Liquid Die Springs, Vibration Dampers, Machined Springs, Custom Shock and Vibration Isolators, and Custom Actuators, serving aerospace, defense, industrial, and structural markets.

products.name: Seismic Dampers
products.description: Devices designed to mitigate earthquake effects on structures; represent a substantial portion of company sales.

products.name: Fluidicshoks
products.description: Compact shock absorbers up to 19,200 inch-pound capacity in 12 standard sizes for defense, aerospace, and commercial industries.

products.name: Crane and Industrial Buffers
products.description: Large shock absorbers up to 10,890,000 inch-pound capacity in 50+ sizes for cranes, truck docks, and industrial applications.

products.name: Self-Adjusting Shock Absorbers
products.description: Automatically adjust to different impact conditions; designed for high-cycle heavy industry applications.

products.name: Liquid Die Springs
products.description: Component parts used in machinery and equipment for manufacture of tools and dies.

products.name: Vibration Dampers
products.description: Control response of electronics and optical systems subjected to air, ship, or spacecraft vibration in aerospace and defense.

products.name: Machined Springs
products.description: Precisely controlled mechanical springs for aerospace applications requiring custom features unavailable in conventional wound coil springs.

products.name: Custom Shock and Vibration Isolators
products.description: Configurations including liquid springs, fluid dampers, elastomeric springs, and Pumpkin Mounts for defense applications.

products.name: Custom Actuators
products.description: Gas-charged, high-pressure actuators with custom features for special aerospace and defense applications.
